item.title: Hungary Looks to June for Potential Rate Move on Strong Forint
item.description: The forint’s post-election rally should give the central bank an opportunity to reassess its cautious monetary policy, Vice Governor Zoltan Kurali said, signaling that an interest-rate cut as early as June could be on the cards.

item.title: Merz Slams UniCredit for Destroying Trust With Commerzbank Bid
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz issued a blunt criticism of UniCredit SpA’s bid for Commerzbank AG, saying the takeover effort destroys trust.

item.title: Fed’s Hammack Says FOMC Signal on Rate Path Was Misleading
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said language in policymakers’ post-meeting statement suggesting the central bank’s next move would be a cut didn’t match her outlook.

item.title: Fed Needs Policy Clarity to Preserve Independence, G-30 Report Says
item.description: The Federal Reserve needs to improve its execution of monetary policy and how it communicates with investors and the public if it is to protect its independence against ongoing political attacks, according to a think tank of former and current policymakers.

item.title: The Messy, Humiliating Courtroom Drama Between Elon Musk and OpenAI
item.description: Artificial intelligence companies were already unpopular before the billionaires started fighting in court.

item.title: Kushner-Backed Israeli Firm Moves to Defend Its Brightline Bonds
item.description: An Israel-based firm has amassed a sizable stake in the corporate bonds of Florida’s Brightline rail project and is gearing up for workout talks, as the cash-strapped venture struggles under its debt pile.

item.title: Centuries-Old UK Tin Mine Sells 13.5% Bonds to Fund Revival
item.description: A bid to restart tin mining in southwestern England, reviving an industry with roots in the Bronze Age, has won a $210 million vote of confidence from bond buyers.

item.title: PE Firm Hg Marks Down Fund Value by 9% After Software’s Plunge
item.description: Private equity firm Hg marked down the portfolio value of one of its funds by 9% in the first quarter after software valuations fell to a 20-year low, highlighting concerns that artificial intelligence will deal a huge blow to the industry.

item.title: Google Launches $100 Fitbit Air Without a Screen to Rival Whoop
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google on Thursday launched the Fitbit Air, a $100 screenless fitness band aimed squarely at taking on Whoop Inc. and other makers of health and fitness wearables that forgo displays.

item.title: Kalshi Secures $22 Billion Valuation in Coatue-Led Round
item.description: Kalshi Inc. has completed a funding round that values the prediction markets platform at $22 billion, roughly doubling the previous valuation it secured five months ago.

item.title: Poland’s Glapinski Says Rate Hikes More Likely, Not Certain
item.description: Poland’s central bank Governor Adam Glapinski said the likelihood of interest rate increases has grown over the past month although a hike is not a forgone conclusion for policymakers.

item.title: Citi Economist Urges CFA Franc Devaluation to Spur Growth in Central Africa
item.description: Citigroup Inc. says central Africa’s monetary bloc should consider devaluing its currency, which is pegged to the euro, in order to boost economic growth and stem a slide in reserves.

item.title: Brazil Police Target Ex-Bolsonaro Minister in Banco Master Probe
item.description: Brazilian police are targeting Ciro Nogueira, a powerful senator and ally of former President Jair Bolsonaro, as part of their probe into Banco Master, expanding a sweeping investigation that has roiled the nation’s political system.

item.title: Datadog Shares Soar by Most in Six Years on Higher Outlook
item.description: The shares of Datadog Inc. surged by the most in more than six years after the software developer raised its full-year outlook for sales and earnings, far exceeding Wall Street’s expectations.

item.title: Citi Sees Oil ‘Moving Around Like Crazy’ in Hope-and-Fear Dance
item.description: Oil prices will continue to fluctuate wildly until it’s clear whether Iran and President Donald Trump will strike an agreement to end the war, according to Citigroup Inc.’s global head of commodities research.

item.title: Bolivia Is Tapping Global Bond Markets for First Time Since 2022
item.description: Bolivia is returning to international capital markets for the first time in four years, as its new market-friendly government seizes on a tightening sovereign spread to raise fresh financing.

item.title: Germany Sees €52 Billion Tax Hole as Iran War Hits Economy
item.description: Germany expects federal tax revenue to plummet by more than €50 billion ($58.9 billion) through 2030 compared to estimates in October as the government laid the blame with US President Donald Trump’s war in Iran.

item.title: Activist Investor OneMove Says Sylogist Board Blocked Settlement Talks
item.description: Investment firm OneMove Capital Ltd. said Sylogist Ltd.’s board rebuffed attempts to settle a proxy fight, even after the activist investor offered to accept fewer independent director seats.

item.title: Chile Central Bank Says Its Rate Hold Plans Are Still in Effect
item.description: Chile central bankers said their strategy to hold the interest rate steady for a prolonged period remained in effect despite uncertainty from the Middle East war, according to the minutes to their last policy meeting.

item.title: Six Flags Shares Rally as Rising Attendance Drives Revenue Beat
item.description: Six Flags Entertainment Corp. shares rallied after the company’s first-quarter revenue exceeded estimates, driven by rising attendance.

item.title: How to Invest During a Trump Presidency
item.description: See what ETF data says about investing in a chaotic political environment, and why markets feel more volatile than ever.

item.title: US Initial Jobless Claims Tick Up After Falling Near Decades Low
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits rebounded slightly after falling in the previous week to near the lowest levels in decades, signaling layoffs remain muted despite recent job-cut announcements.

item.title: US Productivity Growth Cools on Rebound in Hours Worked
item.description: US labor productivity continued to rise in the first quarter, though at a slower pace, indicating companies are gradually improving worker efficiency to mitigate costs.

item.title: Junk-Bond Craze in Emerging Markets Is Strongest in Eight Years
item.description: Global investors are favoring junk-rated bonds over investment-grade debt in emerging markets by the widest margin in eight years, as the winding down of the Iran war revives the hunt for yield.

item.title: Planet Fitness Falls as New Year’s Resolution Period Disappoints
item.description: Planet Fitness Inc. shares slumped after the budget-friendly gym operator cut its full-year revenue outlook, citing weaker-than-expected member sign-ups during the typically busy New Year period.

item.title: Mozambique Hikes Diesel 46% as Africa Faces War-Driven Oil Surge
item.description: Mozambique, one of the world’s poorest countries, raised diesel prices by 46% on Thursday as African governments lift regulated rates to catch up with higher global market prices caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: Brazil House Backs Critical Minerals Bill as Lula Meets Trump
item.description: Brazil’s lower house approved a bill to regulate the exploration of critical and strategic minerals, a step toward realizing the potential of the nation’s vast reserves as President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva prepares to meet Donald Trump.

item.title: BlackRock Private Debt Fund Cuts Asset Value on Loan Markdowns
item.description: BlackRock Inc. cut the value of its publicly-traded private credit fund by about 5%, as it struggled under the weight of troubled loans, markdowns and lower returns.

item.title: Moody’s Says South Africa on Track to Stabilize Debt This Year
item.description: South African government debt is still on track to have peaked despite the Iran war, Moody’s Ratings said in a new report that endorsed the nation’s focus on reform and prudent public finances.

item.title: Poland Backs Joint EU Debt to Help Finance Defense Spending
item.description: Poland supports plans for bond issuance by the European Union to help member states pay for defense spending to ward off threats from Russia, Finance Minister Andrzej Domanski said.
